title: DNA Library Prep  with MGIEasy UDB Universal Library Prep Set
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7aovb","text":"The MGIEasy UDB Universal Library Prep Set(1000022803,MGI Tech) is specifically designed for creating WGS libraries for the MGI high-throughput sequencing platform series. This library prep set is optimized to convert 1-1000ng of fragmented DNA into a customized library. Unique Dual barcode supported double checked barcode split method ensuring sequencing Homogeneity and accuracy.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"879d3","text":"Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7sasi","text":"MGIEasy Dual Barcode Circularization Kit (Cat. No.: 1000020570)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dtnh0","text":"Qubit ssDNA Assay Kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63u55","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cv792","text":"Supplies:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hi4j","text":"Pipette tips (assorted volumes)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2gppt","text":"5ml round-bottom polystyrene tubes, PCR tube, centrifuge tube","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ok46","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49oa","text":"Equipment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4o9lq","text":"Qubit Fluorometer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1uoj4","text":"Vortex","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bj3cp","text":"Micropipettes, various volumes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5e1gb","text":"Microcentrifuge","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d0q3m","text":"Thermocycler ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2elm1","text":"Magnetic rack","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fragmentation
Use Covaris series models to fragment 55 µL gDNA sample into size between 300-500bp.
Transfer all fragmented DNA to a new 1.5mL centrifuge tube. Add TE buffer for a final volume of 100 µL .
End repair and A-tailing
Transfer DNA sample to a new 0.2mL PCR tube and add TE buffer for a total volume of 39.5 µL .
Prepare the End repair and A-tailing Mixture On ice . %s %s
Transfer 10.5 µL of the mixture to the 0.2mL PCR tube from step 2.1. Votex 3 times and centrifuge briefly.
Place the 0.2mL PCR tube into the thermocycler and run the program. %s %s
Brifly centrifuge to collect the solution at the bottom of the tube.
Adapter ligation
Add 5 µL of UDB Adapter to the corresponding sample tube. Vortex it 3 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice .

Prepare the adapter ligation mixture. Vortex it 6 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice . Components Volume 1 DNA Ligase 4 μL 2 Ligation Buffer 21 μL 3 Total 25 μL adapter ligation mixture

Slowly pipette 25 µL of adapter ligation mixture to each sample tube. Vortex it 6 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice . The adapter ligation mixture is highly viscous. Pipette slowly and carefully.
Place the PCR tube(s) into the thermocycler. Run the program with the following conditions. Temperature Time 1 Heated lid(50°C) On 2 23°C 30 min 3 4°C Hold adapter ligation program
When the program is completed, centrifuge the PCR tube(s) briefly and place On ice .
Add 20 µL TE buffer, for a total volume of 100 µL and transfer to a new 1.5mL centrifuge tube.
Cleanup of Adapter-ligated DNA
Add 50 µL of DNA Clean Beads to each sample tube. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate the sample tube at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the sample tube briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 2 to 5 min until the liquid is clear. Carefully remove and discard all the supernatant.
Keep the tube on the magnetic rack, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol to each tube to wash the beads and tube wall. Wait for 00:00:30 . Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

Repeat step 4.4 once. Try to remove all liquid from the tube. If some liquid remains on the tube wall, centrifuge the tube briefly and place it on the magnetic rack for separation.Remove all liquid by using a low-volume pipette.

Keep the tube on the magnetic rack. Open the tube cap and air-dry the beads at Room temperature until no wetness or glossiness is visible on the beads' surface. There should be no visible cracking on the surface of the beads.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 40 µL of TE buffer to elute the DNA. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate the tube(s) at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the tube briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 2 to 5 min until the liquid is clear. Carefully transfer 38 µL of supernatant to a new 0.2 mL PCR tube.
PCR Amplification
Add 50 µL PCR Enzyme Mix to each sample tube.
Add 12 µL of UDB PCR Primer Mix to each sample tube . Vortex 3 times ( 00:00:03 each) and centrifuge briefly to collect the solution at the bottom of the tube. %s %s

Place the PCR tube into the thermocycler. Run the program with the following conditions. Temperature Time Cycles 1 105 ℃ Heated lid on 2 95 ℃ 3min 1 3 98 ℃ 20s 4 60 ℃ 15s 5 72 ℃ 30s 6 72 ℃ 10min 1 7 4 ℃ Hold 1 PCR program
When the program is completed, centrifuge the tube(s) briefly and transfer to a new 1.5mL centrifuge tube.
Cleanup of PCR Product
Add 100 µL of DNA Clean Beads to each sample tube from 5.4. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Centrifuge the sample tube briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 2 to 5 min until the liquid is clear. Carefully remove and discard all the supernatant.
Keep the tube on the magnetic rack, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol to each tube to wash the beads and tube wall. Wait for 00:00:30 . Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

Repeat step 6.4 once. Try to remove all liquid from the tube. If some liquid remains on the tube wall, centrifuge the tube briefly and place it on the magnetic rack for separation. Remove all liquid by using a low-volume pipette.

Keep the tube on the magnetic rack. Open the tube cap and air-dry the beads at Room temperature until no wetness or glossiness is visible on the beads' surface. There should be no visible cracking on the surface of the beads.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 32 µL of TE buffer to elute the DNA. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate the tube(s) at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the tube briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 2 to 5 min until the liquid is clear. Carefully transfer 30 µL of supernatant to a new 1.5mL centrifuge tube.
Incubate the sample tube at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Quantify the dsDNA with Qubit dsDNA-HS Assay Kit. Yeild for PCR products:>1 pmol. Assess the size range of purified PCR products with Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer.
Combined with MGIEasy Dual Barcode Circularization Kit (Cat. No.: 1000020570) for ssCir preparation and further for DNB preparation. Denaturation
Double size selection The following sample use a 60 µL 1st bead selection and a 20 µL 2nd bead selection to target a 280bp size fragement from fragmented DNA( 100 µL ).
DNA Quantification
Add 60 µL of DNA Clean Beads to each sample tube. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

Centrifuge the tube(s) briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 until the liquid is clear. Then, carefully transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5mL centrifuge tube. Retain the supernatant and dicard the beads.
Add 20 µL of DNA Clean Beads to the centrifuge tube with 144 µL supernatant. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the tube(s) briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 until the liquid is clear. Carefully remove and discard all the supernatant.
While keeping the centrifuge tube on the magnetic rack, add 200 µL of 80% ethanol to each tube to wash the beads and tube wall. Wait for 00:00:30 . Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Repeat step 2.8 and try to remove all of the liquid from the tube.

Keep the tube(s) on the magnetic rack. Open the tube cap and air-dry the beads at Room temperature until no wetness or glossiness is visible on the beads' surface. There should be no visible cracking on the surface of the beads.
Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack and add 32 µL of TE to elute the DNA. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate the tube(s) at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the tube(s) briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 2 to 5 min until the liquid is clear. Carefully transfer 30 µL of supernatant to a new 1.5mL centrifuge tube.
Based on the PCR products concentration, add 300 ng of PCR products into a new 0.2 mL PCR tube. If the volume is less than 48 µL , add TE Buffer to make a total volume 48 µL .

Place the PCR tube(s) into the thermocycler. Run the program with the following conditions. Temperature Time 1 100 ℃ Heated lid On 2 95 ℃ 3 min 3 4 ℃ 10 min Denaturation reaction conditions (Volume: 48 μL)
After the reaction, centrifuge the tube briefly and place On ice .
Quantify the purified fragment products with Qubit dsDNA Assay Kit.
Digestion
Cleanup of digestion product
QC of digestion product
Quantify the ssCir with Qubit ssDNA Assay Kit. The final Enzymatic Digestion products (ssDNA, ng) / input products of PCR (dsDNA, 300 ng) should be ≥ 7%.
Mix the DNA Clean Beads thoroughly. Add 130 µL of DNA Clean Beads to each sample tube. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the tube(s) briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 until the liquid is clear. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
While keeping the tube(s) on the magnetic rack, add 160 µL of 80% ethanol to each tube to wash the beads and tube wall. Wait for 00:00:30 . Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

Repeat step 12.4. Try to remove all liquid from the tube. If some liquid remains on the tube wall, centrifuge the tube briefly and place it on the magnetic rack for separation. Remove all liquid by using a low-volume pipette.

Keep the tube(s) on the magnetic rack. Open the tube cap and air-dry the beads at room temperature until no wetness or glossiness is visible on the beads' surface. There should be no visible cracking on the surface of the beads.
Remove the tube(s) from the magnetic rack and add 25 µL of TE Buffer to elute the DNA. Mix with a vortexer until all beads are suspended.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Centrifuge the tube briefly and place on the magnetic rack for 00:05:00 until the liquid is clear. Carefully transfer 24 µL of supernatant to a new 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.
According to the desired reaction number, prepare the digestion mixture in a 0.2 mL PCR tube On ice . Vortex it 3 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice .
Add 4 µL of digestion mixture to each sample tube. Vortex it 3 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and then place On ice .

Place the PCR tube(s) into the thermocycler. Run the program with the following conditions. Temperature Time 1 42 ℃ Heated lid On 2 37 ℃ 10 min 3 4 ℃ Hold Digestion reaction conditions (Volume: 64 μL)

When the program is completed, centrifuge the tube briefly and immediately add 7.5 µL of Digestion Stop Buffer to each sample tube. Vortex it 3 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice .
Single-stranded circularization

According to the desired reaction number, prepare the circularization reaction mixture in a new 0.2 mL PCR tube On ice . Vortex it 3 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice . Reagent Volume per reaction 1 Dual Barcode Splint Buffer 11.6 μL 2 DNA Rapid Ligase 0.5 μL 3 Total 12.1 μL Circularization reaction mixture
Add 12.1 µL of circularization reaction mixture to each sample tube. Vortex it 3 times ( 00:00:03 each), centrifuge briefly, and place On ice .

Place the PCR tube(s) into the thermocycler. Run the program with the following conditions. Temperature Time 1 42 ℃ Heated lid On 2 37 ℃ 10 min 3 4 ℃ Hold Single-stranded DNA circularization reaction conditions (Volume: 60 μL)
When the program is completed, place the PCR tube(s) On ice , centrifuge briefly, and immediately proceed to the next step.
creator: Qianyue Ji
